verses.text: The LORD: hew two tables of stone like the first; I will write on them what was on the first. Be ready in the morning; come up Sinai; present thyself on the top. No man come up with thee. Moses hewed two tables, rose early, went up, took the tables in hand. Guidance: This time Moses carries the stones. The first tables were God's work entirely; the second are jointly made — God's words on Moses' quarrying. After failure, God lets us share the labor again.

The LORD descended in the cloud, proclaimed His name: The LORD, The LORD God, merciful and gracious, longsuffering, abundant in goodness and truth, keeping mercy for thousands, forgiving iniquity, transgression and sin — yet by no means clearing the guilty, visiting iniquity to the third and fourth generation. Guidance: God's own self-portrait. Mercy named first, mercy named last, mercy measured in thousands of generations. The text Israel sings back to itself most often in the Psalms and Prophets is this one.

Moses made haste, bowed to the earth, worshipped. He said: if I have found grace, O Lord, let my Lord go among us — for it is a stiff-necked people. Pardon our iniquity, and take us for Thine inheritance. Guidance: Moses takes God's mercy and pleads it back — yes, we are stiff-necked, but Thou art merciful. The strongest prayer leans on God's just-revealed character. We ask for what He has just shown us He is.

I make a covenant: before thy people I will do marvels not done in all the earth. Drive out the inhabitants. Make no covenant with them — lest a snare. Destroy their altars, break images, cut groves. Worship no other god — the LORD whose name is Jealous is jealous. Take not their daughters for thy sons. Make no molten gods. Guidance: God renews the covenant. The greatest danger ahead is not Canaan's armies but Canaan's altars. Watch the marriages of your children; that is where idolatry walks in.

Feast of unleavened bread seven days in Abib. Every firstborn is Mine. Firstling ass redeem with a lamb. Redeem firstborn sons. None appear before Me empty. Six days work, seventh rest. Feast of weeks; ingathering at year's end. All males appear thrice yearly. No man shall desire thy land when thou goest up. Bring first firstfruits. Seethe not a kid in its mother's milk. Guidance: The yearly rhythm is repeated from Exodus 23. God circles back to feast and firstfruits after every fall. Worship is the route home.

The LORD: write these words — after their tenor I have made a covenant with thee and Israel. He was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; neither ate bread nor drank water. He wrote on the tables the ten commandments. Guidance: Forty days a second time. No food, no water, the same length Jesus would later spend in the wilderness. Some words are only writable after long fasting in the presence of the One who speaks them.

The LORD: write these words — after their tenor I have made a covenant with thee and Israel. He was there with the LORD forty days and forty nights; neither ate bread nor drank water. He wrote on the tables the ten commandments. Guidance: Forty days a second time. No food, no water, the same length Jesus would later spend in the wilderness. Some words are only writable after long fasting in the presence of the One who speaks them.

When Moses came down with the two tables, he wist not that his face shone from talking with God. Aaron and all Israel saw it; they were afraid to come near. Moses called them; Aaron and the rulers returned. Afterward all Israel came near; he gave commandment all the LORD spoke in Sinai. Guidance: Moses doesn't even know he's glowing. Time with God leaves a mark you can't see in yourself. People around you will know before you do where you've been.

When Moses had done speaking, he put a vail on his face. When he went in before the LORD, he took it off till he came out. He spake what was commanded; Israel saw his face shone; he put the vail again. Guidance: Paul takes this up in 2 Corinthians 3: Moses veiled himself because the glory was fading. In Christ, the vail is taken away. We behold His glory unveiled and are transformed from glory to glory.
